items.prompt: Classify the study as survey, observational study, or experiment and state what claim is supported.
items.expected_evidence: compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Use a two-way table or probability model to find the probability of a compound event.
items.expected_evidence: explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Describe the center and spread of a distribution using the given plot.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish observed-versus-expected evidence from stronger causal claims
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates distinguish observed-versus-expected evidence from stronger causal claims with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain how sample design affects what can be inferred about a population.
items.expected_evidence: compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings.

items.prompt: Explain why theoretical probability and short-run experimental results can differ.
items.expected_evidence: explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data.

items.prompt: A student uses an observational study to claim causation. Diagnose the study-design error. Focus on sample space model fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings.

items.prompt: A student adds overlapping events as if they were disjoint. Identify the double-counting mistake. Focus on independence conditioning opacity.
items.expected_evidence: explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data.

items.prompt: Read a less-familiar graph or study summary and decide whether the reported conclusion is supported.
items.expected_evidence: compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Given a probability simulation result, explain whether the difference from theory is surprising or expected.
items.expected_evidence: explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain what a chi-square comparison says about fit or association in categorical data in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, interpret a new sample summary and state one supported inference and one unsupported claim.
items.expected_evidence: compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates compute or interpret expected counts in bounded categorical-data settings on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Run a prerequisite check on probability models and compound events to determine whether the reported error reflects upstream representation limits or current chi square reasoning reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether probability models and compound events is secure enough to support chi square reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in probability models and compound events from a current-node issue in chi square reasoning.

items.prompt: Run a prerequisite check on sampling inference and study design to determine whether the reported error reflects upstream representation limits or current chi square reasoning reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether sampling inference and study design is secure enough to support chi square reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in sampling inference and study design from a current-node issue in chi square reasoning.
